segments.s160-g206: بدینسان is an adverb 'in this way / thus' introducing a result or consequence. بودبند is an archaic fused predicate (here functioning as 'was' + a noun 'bond/connection' or a compound meaning 'was the binding'), بد را کلید is the object phrase with را marking the direct object: 'the key of the bad/evil' (کلید used figuratively). Together: 'Thus this became the key to an evil bond.'
segments.s160-g207: خردمند مردم is a genitival/attributive phrase 'the wise of the people / wise men among the people'. چه is the interrogative 'what' (here 'what will ... say'); گوید is the present/subjunctive 'will say' or rhetorical future; کنون is the adverb 'now' placed at clause end. The clause asks rhetorically what the wise will now say.
segments.s160-g208: خوی شرم is a possessive/nominal phrase 'the habit/disposition of shame' (خوی = habit/disposition, شرم = shame). از + این (here split as از / ین) marks source or cause 'from/ because of this'; داستان = 'tale/story'. گشت is the past of 'to become' (turned into), and خون 'blood' (or 'bloodshed') is the resultant state: literally 'the habit of shame, from this tale, became blood' → 'their shame was turned into blood/bloodshed by this story.'
segments.s160-g121: This clause uses the restrictive preposition 'جز ... از' ('except from') with the possessor phrase 'دختر من' ('my daughter') after the noun, as is normal in Persian ('دختر من' = 'my daughter'). 'پسندش' contains the third-person object clitic -ش referring to the woman in question (literally 'his/her liking' → 'liked her'), and 'نبود' is the past negative copula 'was not' (here functioning as 'there was no liking').
segments.s160-g122: The opening preposition 'ز' (poetic for 'from/of') introduces the group 'خوبان' ('the good/beautiful ones'). 'کسی ... نبود' with the negative copula means 'no one (there) was'. 'ارجمندش' again has the -ش clitic (object 'her') and 'ارجمند' here means 'esteemed/held dear'; 'نبود' negates it in past tense.
segments.s160-g123: 'چنان شاد شد' is a standard adjectival + copular past: 'chonan' = 'so/such', 'shad' = 'happy', 'shod' (شد) = 'became'. 'زان سخن شهریار' ('زان' = archaic/poetic 'از آن' = 'because of that') is an ablative phrase 'at/for that word of the king' giving the cause.
segments.s160-g124: The relative/result marker 'که' here introduces the result clause. 'ماه' ('the moon', used metaphorically for a beautiful person) is the subject; 'آمدش' is the past 'came' with the clitic -ش = 'came to him/her'. 'گفتی' is a noun meaning 'conversation' (poetic usage) implying they spoke, and 'اندر کنار' is an archaic prepositional phrase 'at/beside (his) side'.
segments.s160-g125: 'در گنج بگشاد' is a poetic past construction: 'در' = 'in/into (the) treasure (vault)', and 'بگشاد' is an archaic past form meaning 'opened' (cf. modern 'گشود'/'باز کرد'). The conjunction 'و' links the verb phrase to the quantified noun 'چندان گهر' ('so many jewels').
segments.s160-g126: The source preposition 'ز' ('from') introduces the material/origin: 'دیبای زربفت' = 'gold-brocaded brocade/silk' (zarbaf means woven with gold). The conjunction 'و' links the next item 'زرین کمر' = 'a golden kamar' where 'کمر' here means 'girdle/belt'.
segments.s160-g127: 'همان' ('that same') repeats and points to items already meant; it attaches to a coordinated list 'یاره و تاج و انگشتری' ('beloved/companion, crown, and ring'). In this poetic inventory the nouns are simply conjoined with 'و'.
segments.s160-g128: Again 'همان' repeats 'that same'; 'طوق' = 'necklace/collar'. The second 'هم' (here functioning as 'also/too') links the final item 'تخت گنداوری' — 'تخت' = 'throne' followed by a proper-name-like modifier 'گنداوری' (a named throne). In Persian poetry noun + name forms the composition 'throne of Gandaavari' without an explicit ezafe in this meter.
segments.s160-g49: The clause begins with the relative/introductory particle 'که' introducing a report or indirect question ('what ...'). 'از پیش' is a compound meaning 'previously' ('از' + 'پیش' = 'from before'). 'با شاه ایران' literally 'with the king of Iran' functions as the target/affected person ('toward the king of Iran'). 'چه کرد' ('what did [he]') uses 'چه' as interrogative/relative 'what' and 'کرد' as the past-tense verb 'did'. Together: 'what he had previously done to the king of Iran'.
segments.s160-g50: 'ز' (poetic variant of 'از') marks origin/partitive 'from'. 'گردان ایران' = 'the troop/army of Iran' (noun phrase). 'برآورد' here is a past action 'brought forth/caused to come out' (cf. برآوردن). The final 'گرد' appears as an element of the verbal complex (archaic/poetic finishing particle) and in effect completes the past-action sense — the whole means 'he brought out/raised the Iranian troops.'
segments.s160-g51: 'پر از' = 'full of' (adjective + prepositional phrase). 'بند' here means 'stanza/verse'. 'سودابه' is the proper name; 'کاو' is an archaic item that here connects to the following noun phrase and functions like a relative/clarifying word ('who/which' in older usage), but that exact reading is not among the provided senses (so we mark it as unmatched). 'دخت اوست' = 'she is his daughter' with 'دخت' = 'daughter/maiden' and 'ست' = copula 'is'. The phrase as a whole: 'full of Sudabeh's stanzas; she is his daughter.'
segments.s160-g52: 'نخواهد' is the negative of 'خواهد' (will/wishes) and here conveys 'she does not/will not want'. The enclitic/progressive particle 'همی' is an archaic/poetic marker often signalling continuous or emphatic aspect. 'دوده را' marks 'doodeh' (soot/ash) as the definite object with the object marker 'را'. 'مغز و پوست' are two nouns listed as parts ('core/innards and skin/outer part') joined by 'و' (and). The phrase means 'she will not want (even) the soot, neither its inner nor its outer part.'
segments.s160-g53: 'به پاسخ سیاوش' = 'in answer to Siyavash' with 'به' marking the relation 'to/for' and 'پاسخ' = 'reply/answer'. 'چو' here means 'when'. 'بگشاد لب' is the verb phrase 'opened her lips' where 'بگشاد' is an archaic/poetic past form of 'to open' and 'لب' = 'lip(s)'. Overall: 'when, in reply to Siyavash, she opened her lips.'
segments.s160-g54: 'پری چهره' is a compound epithet 'fair-/fairy-faced' (literally 'fairy-faced one' or 'beautiful-faced woman'). 'برداشت' here is a past-tense verbal use 'took up/withdrew/removed' (lit. 'lifted off' or 'turned away'). 'از رخ قصب' = 'from the face of the reed' or more idiomatically 'from the reed' with 'از' = 'from' and 'رخ' = 'face/cheek' and 'قصب' = 'reed/stalk'; together describing the motion of turning the face away. So: 'the fair-faced woman turned her face away from the reed.'
segments.s160-g55: 'بدو' is an enclitic pronoun 'to him/to her/to it' (here 'to her'). 'گفت' would normally be the past tense verb 'said', but the provided senses list gives lexical senses for 'گفت' as 'conversation/discussion' rather than the verb; the intended reading in the line is the verb 'said' with 'خورشید' (the sun) as subject. 'با ماه نو' here means 'to the new moon' or 'addressing the new moon' with 'با' functioning like a dative/with marker and 'ماه نو' = 'new moon'. The whole: 'the sun said to the new moon.'
segments.s160-g56: 'گر' = 'if'. 'ایدون' is an archaic/poetic form meaning 'come/appear' (present/future sense). 'که' is the subordinating particle 'that' (introducing the content clause). 'بینند' is the present/subjunctive plural 'they see' or 'they are to see'. 'بر گاه نو' = 'at/on the new time/occasion' with 'بر' = 'on/upon', 'گاه' = 'time/occasion' and 'نو' = 'new'. So the clause reads: 'if now/if they come to appear to see at the new time.'
segments.s160-g257: ندید is the past negative of دید ('did not see' / 'did not perceive'). از سیاوش is a prepositional phrase 'from Siavash' (source). بدان گونه بوی is a noun phrase 'that kind of scent' (بدان here functions as an emphatic/demonstrative particle 'in that/that kind of', گونه = 'manner/kind', بوی = 'scent'); the whole segment says he did not perceive that sort of scent coming from Siavash.
segments.s160-g258: نشان is a noun 'a sign/trace'. بسودن appears here as a verbal-nominal form indicating the state/occurrence tied to that sign (contextually 'of that being the case' / 'of it manifesting'); نبود is the past negative copula 'there was not'. اندروی is an adverb 'inwardly/within' placed after the verb phrase for emphasis. Together: 'there was no inward sign of it.'
segments.s160-g259: غمی گشت is an intransitive clause 'it became sorrow' / 'sorrow arose' (گشت = 'became/turned'). و links the next clause. سودابه را خوار کرد is 'caused Sudabeh to be despised' — را marks سودابه as the definite object, خوار is predicate 'despised/humbled', and کرد is the causative/light-verb 'made/caused'.
segments.s160-g260: دل خویشتن را is the object 'his own heart' (دل = 'heart', خویشتن = emphatic 'oneself / his own', را marks it definite). پرآزار کرد is 'made (it) full of torment' — پرآزار is an adjective/compound 'full of distress', and کرد is the verb 'caused/made'. The sequence shows a causative structure: he caused his heart to become tormented.
segments.s160-g261: به دل گفت is an idiom 'he said to (his) heart' — i.e., he told himself. کاین را is the direct object of the reported speech 'this (one)' (کاین = 'this/that'), and به شمشیر تیز is an instrumental phrase 'with a sharp sword' (به marking the instrument here). The segment sets up reported intention: 'he told himself that this one, with a sharp sword...'.
segments.s160-g262: بباید is a modal auxiliary 'must/ought to' requiring an infinitive. کنون is an adverb 'now'. کردنش is the light verb کردن 'to do/make' with the enclitic ش ('her/it') attached — together 'to do her' i.e. 'to deal with her'. ریز ریز is a reduplicated adverb 'piece by piece / in small pieces' emphasizing the manner of action. Grammatically, بباید + کنون + کردنش expresses obligation: 'now must cut her into small pieces.'
segments.s160-g263: ز هاماوران is a prepositional phrase 'from the plains' (ز = 'from', هاماوران = 'level plains/expanses' — here a poetic locative). زان پس is the temporal connective 'after that' (زان = 'from that / therefore', پس = 'after/then'). اندیشه کرد is the past-tense verbal phrase 'he thought / he deliberated'. The sequence gives time/place background and the main verb 'he reflected/considered.'
segments.s160-g264: که introduces a subordinate clause 'that ...'. آشوب is the subject/nominal 'uproar/turmoil'. خیزد is the present-subjunctive/optative form of خاست 'to arise' — here used in indirect discourse after 'he thought' to mean 'would arise'. پرآواز و درد are predicative attributes 'loud-voiced and painful' describing the anticipated turmoil. The clause therefore expresses his fear that a noisy, painful disturbance would begin.
segments.s160-g329: همی (an iterative/adverbial particle) + گفت (past of گفتن) introduces reported speech: “he kept saying.” بنگر is an imperative/quotative verb meaning “behold/look,” followed by the exclamative چه کرد از بدی (“what/what manner of thing she did of evil”); از here marks source/quality (’of’). The whole segment functions as ‘he kept saying, “See what evil she has done.”’
segments.s160-g330: به گفتار او is an instrumental/causal prepositional phrase (‘by/at her speech’). خیره and ایمن are predicative adjectives placed before the verb شدی; خیره = ‘astonished/staring’ and ایمن = ‘safe/assured.’ شدی is 2nd-person past (become) so the clause reads ‘at her words you became astonished and felt secure/assured.’
segments.s160-g331: دل شاه کاووس is a possessive/genitive sequence ‘the heart of King Kavus’ (دل + apposition شاه کاووس). شد is the past of شدن, here ‘became,’ and بدگمان is a predicative adjective ‘suspicious/distrustful.’ The clause means ‘the heart of King Kavus became suspicious.’
segments.s160-g332: برفت is a past-tense verb ‘he departed/rose/went off.’ و simply connects clauses. در اندیشه شد is a light-verb construction: در + noun (اندیشه) + شد = ‘fell into thought / became thoughtful.’ یک زمان is an adverbial phrase ‘for a moment / for a time.’
segments.s160-g333: همی گفت again introduces reported speech (‘he kept saying’). کاین is the fused classical form (که + این) meaning ‘this’ (or ‘that this’); را is the direct-object marker. چه درمان کنم is a rhetorical question: چه = ‘what’ and درمان = ‘remedy/treatment’; کنم is 1st-person present/subjunctive of کردن used here as ‘shall I do / can I do.’ The clause reads ‘he kept saying, “What remedy shall I do for this?”’
segments.s160-g334: نشاید is an impersonal/modal negative meaning ‘it is not fitting / it should not be.’ که introduces the subordinate clause. این بر دل آسان کنم uses بر دل (‘upon the heart’) and آسان (‘easy’) with کنم (1st-person subjunctive) to mean ‘that I should make this easy on the heart’ — i.e. ‘it would not be right for me to ease this matter for the heart.’
segments.s160-g335: ازان پس is a temporal phrase ‘after that / thereafter.’ نگه کرد is a simple past construction literally ‘made/looked’ used here as ‘kept watch / looked’ (کرد combines with the noun نگه to form the verbal idea). کاووس شاه is the name with title ‘King Kavus.’
segments.s160-g336: کسی را که introduces a relative clause with را marking the person as definite object (‘the person whom…’). کردی is 2nd-person past (‘you did/made/appointed’). به اختر نگاه is a purposive/instrumental phrase: به + اختر (‘to/at the star’) + نگاه (gaze/look) — together ‘to look/watch the star.’ The relative clause thus: ‘the person whom you had made to watch the star.’
segments.s160-g361: بنالید is a past/continuous narrative verb (بـ- prefix + nalیدن) meaning 'moaned/wailed.' سودابه is the proper name in the oblique position following the verb (common in older Persian word order: verb before subject). و simply connects the two coordinated actions; داد here is the noun 'justice' (not the verb 'gave') and خواست is the verb 'demanded/requested' in past tense, so داد خواست together mean 'demanded justice.'
segments.s160-g362: ز is the poetic preposition 'from/unto' indicating the addressee; شاه جهاندار is a compound title 'the king, the sovereign' (شاه = king, جهاندار = sovereign/ruler). فریاد is a noun 'cry/outcry' and خواست is again the past verb 'requested/cried out (to)' — together indicating she 'cried out to the sovereign.' Note the verb خواست governs the preceding noun phrase as its object/goal.
segments.s160-g363: همی is a frequentative/adverbial particle meaning 'continually/kept on'; گفت here stands for the past-tense verb 'said' (older narrative word order places گفت after همی). همداستانم is a first-person predication 'I am of the same mind / I agree' (ـم is the 1st-person ending). ز شاه marks the thing/person she agrees with ('with/from the king') using the poetic preposition ز.
segments.s160-g364: This clause lists the actions she supports: به introduces verbal nouns or infinitival senses ('to/for'); زخم is 'wounding' (literally 'wound'); و links the items; به افگندن uses the infinitival/nominal form 'to cast/throw' (افگندن = to cast down); از marks the source 'from'; تخت is 'throne' and گاه here functions as 'seat/place/occasion' (older usage) — together: 'for wounding and for casting from the throne and seat.'
segments.s160-g365: ز is the causal/prepositional 'because of'; فرزند کشته is a noun phrase 'the slain child' (کشته is a past participle modifying فرزند). بپیچد is an imperfective/present-form verb with the prefix بـ (here third-person singular) meaning 'twists/turns/writhes' (a physical-metaphorical reaction). دل + ـم yields 'my heart' (دل = heart, ـم = 1st-person clitic).
segments.s160-g366: زمان تا زمان is a repetitive/time phrase 'time after time / from moment to moment' (زمان ... تا ... gives the 'until/throughout' sense). سر ز تن is a common formulaic object 'the head from the body' (سر = head; ز = from; تن = body). بگسلم is a volitional/imperfective-stative form meaning 'I would rend/tear off' (here hyperbolic: 'I would tear my head from my body'). The structure expresses repeated intense grief.
segments.s160-g367: بدو is the prepositional particle 'to him/to her' (literally 'unto him') marking the indirect object; گفت is the past 'said' (he said to her). ای زن is a vocative 'O woman' (addressing her); تو is an emphatic 'you'; آرام گیر is an idiomatic imperative 'be calm / keep quiet' (آرام = calm; گیر here functions as an imperative or stative command 'take/hold yourself calm' in older usage).
segments.s160-g368: چه گویی is a rhetorical present interrogative 'what/why are you saying' (چه as exclamatory 'what'; گویی is the present/subjunctive-form of گفتن used to mean 'you say' or 'you are saying'). سخنهای is the plural/construct form 'words/speeches' and نادلپذیر is an adjective 'unpleasant / unacceptable'; together: 'what are you saying — unacceptable words?' (a reproach).
segments.s160-g217: چرا (why) introduces a rhetorical question; خواندم is a 1st-person past tense verb 'I called/summoned' (here 'خواندم' = 'I summoned'); در شبستان ترا is a prepositional phrase 'into/inside the inner chamber' with ترا = ت (2nd-person object suffix) + را (object/topic marker) attached to indicate 'you' as the one summoned.
segments.s160-g218: این عبارت خبری است: کنون 'now' sets the time; غم مرا بود is an ergative-style possession formula 'sorrow was to me' (مرا here marks the dative/possessive 'to me'); بود is the past copula 'was'; و links the next phrase; دستان ترا is a noun phrase 'your hands' (دستان 'hands', ترا = ت + را again marking 'your'/'you' as object/agent in classical order).
segments.s160-g219: کنون 'now' sets time; راستی جوی is a compound imperative/command sense 'seek the truth' where راستی = 'truth' and جوی = 'seek/search' (here verbal noun/imperative sense); و با من بگوی is an imperative phrase 'and speak with me' (با = 'with', من as object 'me', بگوی is an imperative/hortative form meaning 'speak/tell me' in older/registered Persian).
segments.s160-g220: سخن 'speech/tell' introduces reported content; بر چه سانست is an idiomatic classical question 'as to what manner/how is it' (بر = prepositional marker 'about/toward', چه = interrogative 'what/how', سانست = older fused form meaning 'it is of such a nature'); بنمای is an imperative 'show/reveal!', and روی is the direct object 'face' — together 'tell what it is like and show your face.'
segments.s160-g221: سیاووش is the speaker's name; گفت here is the reporting verb 'said' (classical narrative past); آن کجا رفته بود is an indirect question 'where had he gone' — آن is a deictic 'that/then', کجا 'where', رفته past participle 'gone', بود past auxiliary 'had been' (past-perfect sense in classical Persian).
segments.s160-g222: وزان در begins with وزان = 'and from that' (connecting phrase); در 'in/at' introduces the location; که here introduces a relative/subordinate clause 'where' or 'that'; سودابه آشفته بود is 'Sudabeh was distressed' (آشفته = 'disturbed/upset', بود = past 'was').
segments.s160-g223: چنین گفت سودابه is a narrative formula 'thus said Sudabeh' (چنین = 'thus/so', گفت = 'said', سودابه = name). کاین نیست راست is a classical negation/emphasis 'this is not true' where کاین = 'that/this' (fused classical demonstrative), نیست = 'is not', راست = 'true' (used as emphatic predicate).
segments.s160-g224: که introduces the content clause 'that ...'; او 'he' is the subject; از بتان 'from the betan' literally 'from the idols/beautiful ones' (here poetic 'betan' = beloveds/beauties rather than literal idols); جز 'except'; تن من 'my body' (تن = body, من here functions as the possessor/'me'); نخواست is past negative 'he did not want/ask for' (ن- prefix + خواست 'wanted').
segments.s160-g249: ببینم is an optative/volitive present form meaning “let me see” or “I wonder.” کزین introduces the subordinate clause (‘from these’/‘of these’) and precedes the noun phrase دو گنهکار (‘two sinners’). کیست is the interrogative clause-final copula (کی + ست), with کی = “who” and ست = “is.” Word order: main verb (ببینم) + subordinate clause introduced by کزین.
segments.s160-g250: This is a second interrogative clause. ببادافره is a participial/phrase meaning ‘to be blown away / ruined by the blast (of wind)’ and functions as the predicate complement; بد modifies سزاوار to mean ‘deserving of ill/evil.’ سزاوار is a predicative adjective ‘deserving,’ and کیست again asks ‘who is (so).’ The clause literally asks who among them is worthy to be thus ruined.
segments.s160-g251: بدان here means ‘to that’ / ‘for that purpose’ (به + آن) and introduces the purpose of the following infinitive. بازجستن is the infinitive ‘to examine / to inquire into.’ همی is an aspectual adverb marking continued or habitual action. چاره جست is a compact past-tense clause: چاره ‘a remedy/means’ as object and جست (past of جستن) ‘he sought.’ Order: purpose phrase + infinitive phrase, then the main clause ‘he sought a remedy.’
segments.s160-g252: ببویید is a past-form meaning ‘they smelled / they perceived the scent’ (classical narrative past). دست سیاوش is the direct object with the possessor after the noun (classical order: ‘the hand of Siyavash’). نخست is an adverb ‘first’ placed after the object. The clause reads ‘first they smelled Siyavash’s hand.’
segments.s160-g253: This is a coordinated list of body-parts/locations: بر (here functioning as a locative/part reference ‘on / upon’), و (= and), بازو ‘arm,’ و (and), سرو ‘cypress’ (a conventional simile for stature/neck). بالای او ‘above him’ / ‘the high part of him’ attaches to the final item to indicate location. The phrase enumerates where the scent was found.
segments.s160-g254: سراسر is an adverb ‘throughout’ or ‘all over.’ ببویید (past) again ‘they smelled / perceived the scent.’ هرجای او ‘everywhere of him’ / ‘in every place about him’ specifies the extent. Combined: ‘they perceived the scent all over, everywhere around him.’
segments.s160-g255: ز is the poetic preposition ‘from.’ ز سودابه indicates source (‘from Sudabeh’ or ‘from Sudabeh’s person’). بوی is the noun ‘scent.’ می‌و here joins with مشک ناب as a coordinated list ‘(the) scent of wine and pure musk’ — می‌و representing the wine/fruit sense in older spelling, مشک ناب = ‘pure musk.’ The whole is a genitive-type phrase: ‘the scent from Sudabeh: wine and pure musk.’
segments.s160-g256: همی before the verb marks continuous or habitual past aspect (‘kept on / continually’). یافت is the past tense ‘he found / perceived.’ کاووس is the subject (Kavus). بوی گلاب is the object ‘the scent of rosewater.’ Word order places the verb before the subject (a classical poetic order): ‘Kavus continually perceived the scent of rosewater.’
segments.s160-g297: مگر introduces a conditional/exclusive clause here: 'unless'. کین is the abstract noun 'vengeance' and شود is a present/future-like verbal form meaning 'happens/is carried out' (classical Persian uses شود for an event becoming true). بر + سیاوش marks the target 'against Siavash'. درست is postpositive and qualifies how the vengeance should be done: 'properly/rightly.' Together the segment means 'unless vengeance is properly carried out against Siavash.'
segments.s160-g298: کنون is the temporal adverb 'now'. چاره این literally places demonstrative after noun (poetic order): 'this remedy/solution.' ببایدت is a fused, older modal form meaning 'it is necessary for you' / 'you must' (modal obligation). جست is the lexical verb 'to seek' (infinitive) that follows the modal. The whole clause: 'now you must seek this remedy.'
segments.s160-g299: گرین here functions as a poetic contraction meaning 'گر این' ('if this'); it introduces the conditional. نشنوی is 2nd-person present negative ('you do not hear/heed'). آب من is a figurative collocation: 'my آب' = 'my standing/reputation' (آب used figuratively). نزد شاه 'in the presence of the king' / 'before the king' (نزد + person). The clause states the conditional: 'If you do not hear this, my reputation before the king ...'.
segments.s160-g300: شود is the present/future-like verb 'becomes' ('will grow'). تیره is an adjective 'dark/bleak' modifying آب (understood from previous segment). و links the two effects. دور here is 'distant' and مانم is first-person present/subjunctive 'I remain / I shall remain' (poetic order puts verb after predicate). ز گاه 'from the court/from the place/time' uses poetic preposition ز + گاه to indicate separation or removal. Together: 'my standing will become dark, and I shall remain distant from the court.'
segments.s160-g301: بدو is the prepositional phrase 'to him' (بِ + او → بدو). گفت is the verb 'said' (here used as past tense). The word order is inverted: object/indirect object phrase ('to him') + verb + subject ('the woman'). من ترا بنده‌ام is direct speech: من = 'I', ترا (تو + را) = 'to you' (accusative/emphatic), بنده‌ام = 'I am a servant' (present copulaised form). The clause therefore reports: 'The woman said to him, "I am your servant."'
segments.s160-g302: بفرمان is a prepositional phrase 'by/at (your) command' (classical b- + فرمان). و links the two nouns/phrases. رایت is 'the standard/banner' (her standard). سرافگنده‌ام is a stative predicate meaning 'I am ashamed/disgraced' (first person). Together in the reported speech it gives two linked statements of subordination and distress: 'at your command, and my banner is disgraced.'
segments.s160-g303: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. شب تیره شد is a past clause 'the night became dark' (تیره + شد). داروی خورد زن follows with typical classical word order: indirect object/thing 'داروی' (the medicine/drug) + خورد (past of خوردن 'took/ate/swallowed') + زن (the woman) — 'the woman took/swallowed the medicine.' The clause sets the time and the action she took.
segments.s160-g304: که introduces a result or explanatory clause ('so that' / 'and'). بفتاد is past tense of افتادن used impersonally/causatively here: 'befell/occurred' or idiomatically 'a child fell' = 'a child was born/appeared.' زو is the poetic contraction of 'از او' = 'from her.' بچه اهرمن is a noun phrase 'a child of Ahriman' (i.e., a devil-child). The segment states the result: 'and from her there befell a devil-child.'
segments.s160-g513: به نیروی یزدان is an instrumental prepositional phrase: به (here = “by”) + نیروی (ʼstrength/powerʼ) + یزدان (ʼGodʼ), literally “by the power of God.” نیکی دهش is a request/commandal clause: نیکی (ʼgoodnessʼ) is placed before دهش (an archaic jussive/imperative form of دادن ‘to give’), so the verb is asking/commanding that goodness be given — object-before-verb ordering is common in poetic Persian for emphasis.
segments.s160-g514: کزین is a contracted poetic preposition meaning ‘from this’ (کـ + ز + این). کوه آتش is a genitive phrase ‘mountain of fire’ (noun + modifier). نیابم is the negative subjunctive/jussive form (ن‑ + یابم) meaning ‘may I not find / I should not find,’ and تپش is the object ‘a beating / pulse’ that follows the verb, so the clause reads literally ‘from this mountain of fire may I not find a beating.’
segments.s160-g515: خروشی is the subject/agent noun ‘a roar/commotion.’ برآمد is the past tense verb ‘arose/issued forth.’ ز دشت و ز شهر uses the preposition ز (poetic form of از, ‘from’) twice for rhythm: ‘from the plain and from the city,’ giving the source of the sound.
segments.s160-g516: غم آمد is a simple verbal clause ‘sorrow came’ (آمد = past ‘came/happened’). جهان را uses the object/topic marker را to mark ‘the world’ as the affected entity (literally ‘sorrow came the world’ → ‘sorrow came upon the world’). ازان کار بهر is a causal/relational phrase ‘because of that affair’ (ازان = ‘from/that’, کار = ‘affair/deed’, بهر = ‘for/for the sake of/because of’), explaining the cause.
segments.s160-g517: چو introduces a temporal clause 'when.' از دشت سودابه is a prepositional phrase 'from the plain of Sudabeh' (دشت + proper name in genitive relation), آوا is the noun 'sound/voice' and شنید is the past tense verb 'he heard.' Subject is omitted (third person) and understood from previous context.
segments.s160-g518: برآمد به ایوان is the main clause: برآمد ('he rose/went out') + به ایوان ('to/onto the iwan/portico'). The coordinating و connects the second action آتش بدید ('and saw fire'), where بدید is the past tense 'saw.' Both verbs share the same implied subject.
segments.s160-g519: همی before a past verb (خواست) is an archaic/progressive marker meaning a continuing or habitual past action — “kept wishing.” کاو را is the object phrase marked by را; here کاو functions as an old/pronoun-like reference to the person who would be harmed (literally “to him/that one”). بد آید is a classical formula meaning “ill/harm should come” (آید here = “happen/befall”), and بروی is the enclitic locative/combinatory form (classical بر وی) that links the happening to that person: “befall him.”
segments.s160-g520: همی بود is again the continuous past copula “was (continually).” جوشان is a predicative adjective meaning “boiling/seething” (emotional intensity). پر از is the fixed prepositional phrase “full of,” and گفت و گوی is a coordinated noun phrase “talk and saying” i.e. “conversation/discussion.” Together: “he was seething, full of talk.”
segments.s160-g401: ز بیم = a causal prepositional phrase 'because of fear'; ز is the poetic preposition 'because of/from' placed before بیم 'fear'. سپهبد گو پیلتن is a title + name: سپهبد (spahbed) 'the general' followed by a name element گو پیلتن (here treated as a name), so the whole chunk means 'the spahbed Go‑Piltan'. Word order is typical: cause ('because of fear of X') comes first.
segments.s160-g402: بلرزد همی شیر در انجمن is a simple clause: بلرزد is third‑person singular present (the present stem with the prefix بـ indicating ongoing/habitual action) 'trembles', همی is a continuous/habitual adverbial particle 'continually/indeed', شیر is the subject 'the lion', and در انجمن is a locative phrase 'in the assembly'. The verb comes before the subject and locative phrase, a common ordering in classical Persian.
segments.s160-g403: کجا زور دارد is a rhetorical interrogative: کجا 'where' (= 'how/where could') + زور دارد 'has strength' (دارد present 'has'). به هشتاد پیل is a prepositional/quantifying phrase 'with/to eighty elephants' (به marking the scope/measure: 'over/as many as eighty elephants'). The phrase asks rhetorically whether anyone could possess such power.
segments.s160-g404: ببندد is third‑person singular present (prefix بـ + present stem + final ـد) meaning 'closes/blocks' or 'binds up'. چو here means 'when' (temporal/conditional), and خواهد is 'he wishes/wants' (present). ره آب نیل is a genitival sequence 'the course (ره) of the water (آب) of the Nile (نیل)' — object of ببندد. Together: 'that he should be able to close the Nile's course whenever he wants.'
segments.s160-g405: همان لشکر نامور صدهزار is a noun phrase: همان 'that/same' (demonstrative pointing back), لشکر 'army' (head noun), نامور 'renowned' (adjective after the noun), and صدهزار 'one hundred thousand' as a numeric postmodifier. Classical Persian commonly places adjectives and numbers after the noun.
segments.s160-g406: گریزند is third‑person plural present 'they flee' (referring to the army). ازو 'from him' (compound of از + سوم شخص enclitic), در صف کارزار 'in the battle‑line' (در = in; صف = battle line/formation; کارزار = battle). The verb‑first order places the action before the source phrase and locative.
segments.s160-g407: مرا نیز پایاب او چون بود is an inverted clause. مرا (here represented by the tokens م + را) is the object 'me' (مرا = 'me'), and نیز 'also'; پایاب او is the nominal predicate 'his pāyāb' (pāyāb = qanat outlet / irrigated land, here a metaphorical possession of او 'him'); چون بود = 'how/what sort it was' or 'how it was' with چون 'how' and بود past 'was'. The order places the object and adverb before the predicate; the sense is rhetorical: 'was I likewise his irrigated outlet?'. Note: the tokenization shows م and را separately but they form the object marker + pronoun 'مرا'.
segments.s160-g408: مگر introduces an exception/condition here 'unless'. دیده is the subject 'the eye', همواره 'always' modifies the verb, پرخون 'blood‑stained' is a predicate adjective, and بود is the past copula 'was'. Together: 'unless his eye was always bloodied' — giving a grim rationale for the earlier rhetorical question.
segments.s160-g321: غمی گشت is a nominal subject + past verb construction: غمی (a grief) + گشت (became/arose). آن شب sets the time ('that night') and is placed after the verb phrase for poetic word order. نزد هیچ دم is a temporal/prepositional phrase meaning 'at no moment / at every moment' (here emphasizing continuous presence); نزد governs the time expression دم ('moment'), while هیچ negates/quantifies it as 'no'/'not a single'.
segments.s160-g322: This clause continues the subject غمی from the previous segment: به شبگیر functions adverbially ('as/like a night-visitor'), برخاست is a past tense verb 'rose/arose', و links the two verbs, and آمد is another past verb 'came'. دژم is a predicate adjective attached to the subject (sorrow) meaning 'sullen/grim'. The sequence Verb + و + Verb is common in classical Persian to show successive actions.
segments.s160-g323: برانگونه is an adverb meaning 'thus / in that manner' and modifies the following clause. سودابه را uses the object-marker را to mark سودابه as the definite object, and خفته is a predicate adjective/participle 'asleep' that follows the object; دید is the past-tense verb 'saw'. The order Object + predicate + verb is normal in Persian when describing how someone was seen.
segments.s160-g324: سراسر is an adverb/adjective meaning 'throughout / entire' modifying شبستان ('bedchamber'); برآشفته is a prefixed participle/adjective 'agitated/disturbed' placed before the final verb دید ('saw'). The whole phrase is another object + adjective + verb construction: 'he saw the whole bedchamber agitated.'
segments.s160-g325: دو کودک is the noun phrase 'two children'. بران گونه is a compound adverbial phrase 'in that way / thus' (بران + گونه) modifying how they were found. بر طشت زر is a locative phrase 'on a golden basin' with بر indicating 'on/upon' and زر qualifying the bowl as gold.
segments.s160-g326: فگنده is a past participle meaning 'thrown/laid' (they were cast/placed). به خواری uses به plus خواری to indicate state or condition ('in humiliation'); و links the next adjective phrase خسته جگر where خسته describes their condition ('weary') and جگر is used metaphorically as 'heart'/'seat of feeling' (i.e., 'with heavy hearts'). The whole cluster describes their afflicted state.
segments.s160-g327: ببارید is an intransitive past verb here meaning 'fell (drops)' or 'rained' in the sense 'fell from'. سودابه is the subject and از دیده آب is a genitival/partitive phrase 'water/from (her) eyes' where از marks origin and دیده is 'eye'. Together: 'water fell from Sudabeh's eyes' (i.e., she wept).
segments.s160-g328: بدو is the clitic pronoun 'to him' (to that person). گفت is the past verb 'said' (here the lexical senses list gives noun senses, but syntactically it functions as the past-tense verb 'he/she said'). روشن is an adverbial/adjective 'clearly/brightly' modifying the following imperative ببین 'look/see' (second-person imperative). آفتاب is addressed vocatively ('O Sun')—in poetry a person may be called 'sun'—so the whole reported speech is: 'Look clearly, O Sun.'
segments.s160-g113: چو ... رسید is a temporal subordinate clause: چو = 'when' introduces the clause and the past verb رسید (V) closes it (typical SOV/verb‑final order). کی here is the temporal/relative 'when' attached to the proper name (an archaic/poetic usage). در شبستان is a prepositional phrase 'in the bedchamber' placed before the verb.
segments.s160-g114: نگه کرد is a light‑verb construction (noun نگه 'gaze' + کرد 'did') meaning 'he looked'. The following phrase سودابه او را بدید is object (سودابه) + object marker (او را) + past verb بدید 'saw' — the object appears before the verb in this poetic SOV order; را marks the definite/direct object.
segments.s160-g115: بر شاه شد is an impersonal/oblique expression meaning 'it befell/reached the king' (بر + noun + شد 'happened/occurred to'). زان سخن مژده داد: زان سخن 'from that speech' + مژده داد 'gave glad tidings' — poetic inversion places the oblique phrase before the verbal phrase; داد is the past 'gave'.
segments.s160-g116: ز کار سیاوش is a prepositional/genitive phrase 'about/from the deeds of Siavash' (ز = 'from/because of' + کار 'deed' + اسمِ خاص). بسی کرد یاد is another light‑verb construction where یاد 'remembrance' with کرد 'did' yields 'remembered greatly' (بسی = intensive adverb 'much' placed before the verb). Word order is object/adverb before the verb as typical in poetry.
segments.s160-g117: که آمد begins a subordinate temporal clause ('when he came'); آمد is the past 'came'. نگه کرد is again the noun+کرد construction 'he gazed/kept his gaze', and ایوان همه 'the entire iwan/portico' appears before the verb (poetic SOV order) as the object/setting being surveyed.
segments.s160-g118: بتان سیه چشم is a noun phrase 'the black‑eyed beauties' (بتان used poetically for beautiful persons), with the adjective phrase سیه چشم ('black‑eyed') following the noun. کردم رمه uses کردم (first‑person past 'I did/ I made / I found') with رمه 'a flock/throng' as object; in this poetic line the speaker reports perceiving/encountering a throng of those black‑eyed beauties (inversion and terse predicate typical of epic style).
segments.s160-g119: چنان بود is 'it was such' (چنان 'such' + بود past 'was'). ایوان is the subject 'the iwan'. ز بس خوب‌چهر is a causal/qualifying phrase 'because of so many fair‑faced (people)' where ز indicates cause/reason and بس is an intensive 'so/much'. The adjective مرکب خوب‌چهر 'fair‑faced' modifies the implied persons in the iwan.
segments.s160-g120: که here introduces a subordinate clause 'so that / as (they) say'. گفتی is used in a proverbial/sustaining sense ('as is said' / 'as people say'); همی is an adverb 'always/ever', and بارد is a verb meaning 'falls/lets fall' (here in the sense of 'sheds/lets fall its radiance' — an older/poetic verbal form). از ماه مهر = 'from the moon of love' (از = 'from/of', ماه = 'moon', مهر = 'love/affection' used poetically as a luminous beloved).
segments.s160-g313: نهاد is the past tense (3rd sg.) of نهادن 'to place/put' here meaning 'he placed'. اندران is an archaic locative adverb equivalent to modern در آن / در آنجا meaning 'in it' or 'therein'. بچه اهرمن is a noun phrase 'the child (offspring) of Ahriman' that serves as the direct object of نهاد.
segments.s160-g314: خروشید is a past-tense verb (3rd sg.) from خروشیدن 'to cry/roar' — 'it cried/roared'. و is the coordinating conjunction 'and'. بفگند is a past/action-form of فگندیدن (with the prosthetic ب-), here 'it hurled/threw' or 'it flung (itself/its contents)'; بر introduces the target 'on/upon'. جامه تن is a genitive-like compound 'the garment of the body' = 'the body's garment/robe'.
segments.s160-g315: دو کودک is the plural subject 'two children'. بدیدند is 3rd-person plural past of دیدن 'they saw'. مرده functions as the direct object meaning 'the corpse'. به طشت here uses به to mark location 'in/into the basin' (classical usage instead of modern در طشت).
segments.s160-g316: از is the preposition 'from' and ایوان is the source/place 'the iwan/porch'. به is the preposition 'to/toward' and کیوان is used poetically for the sky/heaven (literally Saturn). فغان is the noun 'a cry/lament' which is the subject, and برگذشت is the past tense 'passed/was carried' (3rd sg.) — so 'a cry passed from the iwan to the sky.'
segments.s160-g317: چو is the conjunction 'when'. بشنید is 3rd-person past of شنیدن 'he heard'. کاووس is the person (Kavus). از ایوان marks the source 'from the iwan/porch'. خروش is the thing heard 'the cry/uproar' and functions as the object.
segments.s160-g318: بلرزید is past (3rd sg.) of لرزیدن 'he trembled/shook'. در خواب is a locative phrase 'in (his) sleep' meaning 'while asleep'. و is 'and'. بگشاد here is intended as a past verb 'he opened' (from گشودن), and گوش is the object 'ear' or figuratively 'attention' — 'he opened his ear/attended'. Note: the lemma senses given for بگشاد in the word-list don't include the verbal meaning 'opened', so that sense is not among the provided options.
segments.s160-g319: بپرسید is past tense (3rd sg.) of پرسیدن 'he asked / he inquired'. و is 'and'. گفتند is 3rd-person plural past 'they said / they told', used here to introduce the report; however, the provided sense-list for گفتند lists nominal senses (conversation/dialogue) rather than the verbal 'they said', so that exact verbal sense isn't in the supplied options. با شهریار means 'to/with the king' (با here marking the addressee).
segments.s160-g320: که introduces the reported clause ('that' / 'how'). چون here functions as 'how' (in what manner). گشت is past of گشتن meaning 'turned / became' (impersonal here: 'fate turned'). بر marks the target/recipient of that turning 'upon/against'. ماه‌رخ is 'moon-faced', an epithet meaning the beautiful/fair-faced person. روزگار is 'fate/fortune' and is the subject that 'turned against' the moon-faced one.
segments.s160-g273: سیاوش is the proper name (Siavash). ازان is a literary/archaic form meaning "of/from that" (از + آن) and introduces the thing he is innocent of (کارِ بد). کارِ بد is a noun phrase "that bad deed/affair"; بی‌گناه is a postpositive adjective meaning "innocent/blameless," so the whole segment says "Siavash, innocent of that bad deed."
segments.s160-g274: خردمندی is the abstract noun "wisdom/prudence." وی here refers back to Siavash ("his"/"he"). بد‌انست is a past tense verb meaning "perceived/realized/considered," and شاه at the end names the agent "the king." Word order is object(s) before verb; literally "(He) the king perceived his wisdom."
segments.s160-g275: بدو گفت is an idiomatic old-fashioned phrase meaning "he said to him" (بدو = به + او). ازین (از + این) = "about/from this"; خود میندیش is an imperative meaning "think for yourself/consider yourself" (میندیش is an imperative form). هیچ after the verb negates/emphasizes "not at all" — so: "Do not think about this yourself / don't trouble yourself with it."
segments.s160-g276: A triad of qualities joined by و: هشیواری (alertness/awareness), رای (here meaning "counsel/plan" rather than mere 'opinion'), and دانش (knowledge). بسیچ is a quantifier postposed to the list meaning "greatly/much/abundant," so the clause praises his plentiful prudence, counsel, and learning.
segments.s160-g277: مکن is a negative imperative "do not [do]." یاد این = "this remembrance/this matter." هیچ intensifies the negation "not at all." و با کس مگوی = "and do not tell anyone," where مگوی is the prohibitive form of گفتن. The whole is an admonition to keep the matter completely to oneself.
segments.s160-g278: نباید is an impersonal modal "one must not/it must not be." که introduces the subordinate clause. گیرد (from گرفتن) here means "take/receive/assume" (third-person present/subjunctive sense used in a jussive clause), and سخن is "speech/words." رنگ و بوی (literally "color and smell") are metaphors for any trace, appearance, or hint; the clause warns that the talk must not acquire any hint/trace.
segments.s160-g279: چو (= چون) is a subordinating conjunction meaning "when." دانست is past-tense "learned/understood." The following clause (سودابه ... گشت خوار) is the content of his knowledge: سودابه (the woman's name), کاو here is a literary/archaic particle used in older Persian to link or indicate 'thereby/thereof' (its ordinary lexical senses in the provided list do not reflect this usage), and گشت خوار = "became despised." So: "When he learned that Sudabeh had become despised..."
segments.s160-g280: همان = "that very [thing]." سرد شد is the compound "became cold" (سرد adjective + شد 'became'), used metaphorically of feelings. بر دل شهریار = "upon the king's heart," i.e. "the king's heart grew cold/ he became coldhearted or aloof." The phrase expresses the emotional effect on the ruler.
segments.s160-g233: ترا (ت + را) is the clitic object 'to/for you' with ت as the 2nd-person clitic attached to the object marker را; بایدم is an archaic first-person form بایَدم (= بایدم) 'I must' and introduces the speaker's obligation. زین میان is a short adverbial phrase 'in/at this middle/point' (زین = 'from this', میان = 'middle'), and گفت بس is reported speech: گفت 'said' plus بس 'enough'. The whole segment functions as 'I must tell you here: "Enough."'
segments.s160-g234: This is a neither...nor clause marked by the repeated negative particle نه. گنجم here functions as 'my treasure' (poetic/genitive-compound), followed by the copular-like locution به کارست 'is of use' (به + کارست = 'is for use/is useful'). بی تو 'without you' heads the second conjunct, and نه کس 'no one' completes it: 'Neither is my treasure of any use, nor is anyone (of use) without you.'
segments.s160-g235: مرا here is the accusative 'me' (م + را) and خواست is the past of خواستن 'sought/attempted' (i.e., 'he sought me' or 'he made for me'). کارد به کاری به چنگ is a compact description: کارد 'knife/dagger', به کاری 'for a deed/with intent to act' (archaic usage), and به چنگ 'into the grip/in hand' — together describing that he took up the knife to act and held it in his hand. Word order is typical older Persian: object (مرا) before verb (خواست), then instrumental phrases.
segments.s160-g236: دو دست 'with both hands' (noun phrase) + اندر آویخت (پِیوندِ فعل) 'hung on / clung to' — آویخت here carries the sense 'to cling/attach oneself'. چون introduces a simile 'like/as', followed by سنگ تنگ 'a hard/tight stone' (تنگ used adverbially/adjectivally to indicate firmness/close grip). The clause describes the manner of seizing: he clung with both hands like a hard stone.
segments.s160-g237: نکردمش is نَکردم + ش (object/clitic) meaning 'I did not do it / I did not obey it' — with فرمان 'command/order' as the thing not obeyed. همی is an archaic particle used for emphasis or continuous aspect here leading into the action that follows; موی من 'my hair' is the direct object of the verb that begins the next segment. Grammatically the clause sets up 'I did not obey his command' and prepares the action done to the speaker (hair being pulled).
segments.s160-g238: بکند is an older/pre-Classical form (with the prefix بـ) of کنَدن/کندن indicating the action 'he pulls/tears' (here past/perfective sense in narrative); و 'and' links clauses; خراشیده شد is a passive/result phrase 'became scratched' (خراشیده 'scratched' + شد 'became/was'), and روی من 'my face' is the affected locus. Together: 'he pulled (my hair) and my face was scratched.'
segments.s160-g239: یکی کودکی دارم اندر نهان is a verbless noun phrase with an explicit possession verb: یکی کودکی ("a certain child") is the object possessed and دارم is the 1st‑person present of داشتن "I have." اندر نهان is a prepositional/adverbial phrase "in secret/hidden away" describing the child's state and comes after the possessed noun (archaic/poetic word order).
segments.s160-g240: ز پشت تو‌ای شهریار جهان is a locative/vocative continuation: ز (poetic preposition) + پشت تو = "from/behind your back" (an idiom meaning "behind you"). تو‌ای is a vocative address "you — O..." followed by شهریار جهان, the title "sovereign/king of the world." The sequence places the vocative after the locative phrase in elevated poetic style.
segments.s160-g409: جزان = جز آن, a fused archaic construction meaning "except (for)"; کاو here functions as a relative particle (کآو = "that which"), not the lexical verb 'to search', so it introduces the clause "that the astrologer ...". بفرماید is third-person present/optative of فرماییدن (here "he declares / commands"); اخترشناس is the agent "the astrologer." Word order is poetic (relative + verb + agent).
segments.s160-g410: چه گوید سخن = literal order "what (چه) he utters (گوید) — words (سخن)"; وز که دارد سپاس is a relative/qualifying tail meaning "which has praise" (وز here is an archaic/invariant connector rather than the common lexical senses). دادر/دارد is possession "has" and سپاس = "praise." The clause asks rhetorically whether any utterance of his deserves praise.
segments.s160-g411: تراگر = ترا اگر = conditional "if (for) you"; غم = "sorrow"; خرد here is an adjective modifying فرزند (خردِ فرزند = "a small/young child"); نیست negates existence/possession: "there is no sorrow (for you) for the young child." The verb نیست comes at the end as usual in Persian.
segments.s160-g412: مرا هم فزون از تو پیوند نیست = lit. "for me also no bond is more (than you)." مرا (me) is the object (written here split into م + را), هم = "also/too," فزون = "more/greater," از تو sets the standard of comparison "than you," پیوند = "bond/tie," and نیست negates. The clause means "I too have no closer bond than (that with) you."
segments.s160-g413: سخن‌گر = "speaker/orator"; گرفتی is second-person past (تو گرفتی) "you took/treated/considered" (poetic usage allows 'take' = 'treat'); چنین سرسری = "so/thus trivial/cursory." Together: "You treated the speaker so trivially."
segments.s160-g414: بدان = به آن ("by/with that") — a prepositional phrase linking to what precedes; گیتی = "the world"; افگندم is first-person past of افکندن "I cast/threw" (here: "I cast/issued"); این داوری = "this judgment/verdict" is the object. The verb is past and takes the object after it in poetic inversion.
segments.s160-g415: ز دیده = "from the eyes" (ز = from, دیده = eye); فزون زان = "more than that/so much more" (فزون = abundant/more, زان = از آن/than that); ببارید (past) = "(it) rained/fell" and آب = "water." This is poetic word order: object and modifiers appear before the verb.
segments.s160-g416: که introduces the subordinate/comparative clause (here "so that/which"); بردارد is third-person present "(it) takes up/raises" (the sun evaporating or drawing up water); از رود نیل = "from the river Nile" (از = from, رود = river, نیل = Nile); آفتاب = "the sun," the agent performing بردارد. The clause completes the comparison: the tears exceed what the sun draws from the Nile.
segments.s160-g337: بجست is a past-tense narrative verb meaning 'sought' (here: looked for/selected). The conjunction و links it to the following clause. ز + ایشان = 'from among them' (ز used poetically to mark source). بر خویش خواند literally 'called to himself' — خویش here functions as a reflexive ('to his own/self'), formed from خوی + ش; خواند is a transitive past verb 'called/summoned', so the whole phrase means 'he called one of them to himself.'
segments.s160-g338: بپرسید is the past narrative 'he asked' (3rd person). The conjunction و links the action that follows. بر تخت زرین is a locative phrase 'upon the golden throne' (بر = on/upon, تخت = throne, زرین = golden). نشاند is an archaic/transitive past form meaning 'caused to sit / set (someone) down' — here the king causes the person to be seated on the throne.
segments.s160-g339: ز here is the poetic preposition marking the topic or source ('concerning/about' / 'from'). سودابه is the proper name 'Sudabeh'. و links the two nouns. رزم هاماوران is a genitive or noun phrase 'the battle of Hamavaran' or more literally 'battle of the plains' (رزم = battle, هاماوران = 'the level plains' / a place name).
segments.s160-g340: سخن گفت is a light-verb construction: سخن (speech/talk) + گفت (past of گفتن) → 'he spoke/held discourse'. هرگونه is an adverbial phrase 'in every way / of every kind' modifying how he spoke. با مهتران is a prepositional phrase 'with the chiefs/elders' (با = with; مهتران = plural of مهتر, 'chiefs'). Word order places the noun 'سخن' before the verb 'گفت', a common Persian pattern for verbal nouns.
segments.s160-g341: بدان here is a compound built from the prefix/particle ب- plus دان (to know), functioning like 'by/with that' or 'so that they know'. تا introduces a purpose/result clause 'so that / in order that'. شوند is the 3rd-person plural subjunctive/present of شدن 'to become' (here 'may become / would become'). آگه از کار اوی = 'aware of his deed' where از marks the subject-matter ('of'/'concerning') and اوی is the possessive/reflexive 'his'. Overall: 'so that they may become aware of his deed.'
segments.s160-g342: بدانش is a resumptive/demonstrative compound ('by that' / 'thereby'); it links the previous clause to the following. بدانند is 3rd-person plural present/subjunctive of دانستن 'to know' (optative/indicative sense here: 'they would/should know'). کردار اوی = 'his deed/conduct' (کردار = deed, اوی = his). The pair emphasizes that by the foregoing they will come to know his actions.
segments.s160-g343: وزان is an archaic linking particle 'and from that / from those' that ties the remark to what was just said. کودکان is the plural object 'the children'. نیز is 'also / too'. بسیار گفت = 'spoke much / said many things' (بسیار modifying the verb گفت). The overall sense: 'he also said a great deal about those children.'
segments.s160-g344: همی داشت is a past-continuous construction: همی (still/continually) + داشت (past auxiliary meaning 'kept / was keeping') expressing ongoing action. پوشیده is a past participle/adjective 'hidden/covered'. اندر نهفت is a locative + participle 'kept/hidden inwardly' (اندر = in/within; نهفت = concealed). Together they mean 'he continually kept it hidden inside / he kept it secret.'
segments.s160-g425: چنین (an adverb) = 'thus/so' placed before the verb for emphasis; گفت is the past-tense verb 'said' (classical Persian word order freely places the adverb before the verb); موبد is the agent (the priest) followed by the prepositional phrase به شاه جهان 'to the king of the world' where به marks the indirect object and شاه جهان is a titling compound ('king of the world').
segments.s160-g426: که introduces a reported subordinate clause 'that…'; درد سپهبد is the subject phrase 'the spahbed's pain' (درد = pain/affliction + سپهبد as possessor); نماند is the present/past classical verbal form meaning 'does not remain / will not remain' (here stating that it will not stay concealed); نهان is predicate/adverb 'hidden'.
segments.s160-g427: چو خواهی که ... کنی is a conditional construction: چو 'if/when', خواهی (2nd-person singular present of خواستن) 'you want', که introduces the subordinate purpose/content, and پیدا کنی is the subjunctive/infinitival phrase 'to find/discover' with گفت‌وگوی (the conversation) as its object; the object is placed before the verb phrase for metrical/poetic order.
segments.s160-g428: بباید is an impersonal/modal expression 'it is necessary / one must'; it is followed by the verbal noun/infinitive زدن 'to strike', with سنگ را as its definite direct object (را marks the object), and بر سبوی is the prepositional phrase 'upon the ewer' (بر = on/upon + سبوی = ewer in the construct form).
segments.s160-g429: که here introduces a concessive/explanatory clause 'that/although'; هر چند together function as 'however many / although' (هر = every/each, چند = how many / however many) quantifying فرزند 'children'; هست is the copula 'is/are' and ارجمند is the predicate adjective 'esteemed/precious'.
segments.s160-g430: دل شاه = 'the king's heart' (possessive noun phrase with the possessor after the possessed); از اندیشه 'from thought' indicates cause or source of distress; یابد is the classical present-form 'finds/comes upon' (here 'will find' / 'experiences'); گزند is the object/predicate meaning 'harm/damage' — overall: 'the king's heart will suffer harm from thinking/worry.'
segments.s160-g431: وزین here functions as a connective 'and from this' (in the manuscript/poetic idiom) introducing the next topic; دختر شاه هاماوران is a genitive/attributive chain 'the daughter of the king of Hamavaran' (شاه modifies هاماوران as 'king of Hamavaran'). Note: وزین in the lemma list also has adjectival senses, but in context it is the connective/demonstrative.
segments.s160-g432: پر اندیشه = 'full (پر) of thought (اندیشه)' where پر is used as a preposed/complement adjective meaning 'filled with'; گشتی is a verbal form of گشتن here meaning 'became / turned' (classical poetic past/resultive sense), and به دیگر کران = 'to/toward the other shore/edge' (به = to/toward, دیگر = other, کران = edge/shore). The clause thus expresses becoming filled with thought and turning toward the opposite bank.
segments.s160-g281: یکی is an indefinite subject 'someone.' چاره جست is a compact verb phrase where چاره (object 'remedy/solution') comes before the past verb جست ('sought'); اندر آن کار زشت is a prepositional phrase 'in/for that wicked deed' qualifying what remedy was sought.
segments.s160-g282: ز کینه is an adverbial prepositional phrase 'out of/ because of hatred.' درختی is the noun 'a tree' (direct object). بنوی بکشت here is a terse, archaic clustered predicate: بنوی (related to writing) followed by بکشت (a past-form clustered with ب- + کشت/‘he did/caused’), so the sense is 'he effected it by/with writing upon the tree' — i.e. a single action expressed by two short items; the exact lexical pieces are poetic/archaic.
segments.s160-g283: زنی بود is the existential/past 'there was a woman.' با او سپرده treats سپرده ('entrusted thing/person') together with با او to mean 'entrusted to him / in his charge.' درون at the end is a locative adverb 'inside' that prepares the next clause describing what is inside her.
segments.s160-g284: پر از jادوی بود is a copular clause: پر (here 'full') + از ('of') + جادوی ('magic/sorcery') with بود ('was') as the past copula. The coordination و رنگ و فسون lists additional contents: رنگ 'appearance/complexion' and فسون 'enchantment/charm.' The order is adjective/nominal 'full of X and Y' followed by the verb بود.
segments.s160-g285: گران بود is a predicative adjective phrase 'was heavy/weighty' (here describing pregnancy). اندر شکم بچه داشت is a locative + possession sequence: اندر شکم 'in (the) belly/womb' + بچه داشت 'she had a child' (داشت is past 'had'), so together they state she was carrying a child in her womb.
segments.s160-g286: همی is an emphatic/continuative particle often used with past narration; همی از گرانی 'because of the heaviness' uses از + گرانی (گرانی 'weight/heaviness') as the reason phrase. به سختی گذاشت is an adverbial phrase 'she did/put with difficulty' where به سختی = 'with difficulty' and گذاشت (literally 'put/placed') is used idiomatically here in the narrative to indicate the action (i.e. she delivered/gave birth) — an idiomatic, poetic choice of verb.
segments.s160-g287: بدو راز بگشاد is a short clause with the dative pronoun بدو 'to him' followed by راز 'the secret' and بگشاد (from گشودن) meaning 'she opened/revealed' — classical past-form usage. و زو چاره جست links with و ('and') + زو ('from her/of her') + چاره جست 'sought a remedy' (چاره object before the past verb جست). Pronouns and short verbal forms are typical of Shahnameh's elevated, compact style.
segments.s160-g288: کز آغاز پیمانت خواهم نخست is a direct speech / reported intention clause: کز here functions as 'from/of' (كَه + ز contraction) giving 'from the beginning' (کز آغاز). پیمانت is پیمان 'pledge/oath' with the 2nd-person suffix -ت 'your' (possession). خواهم is the 1st-person present/future 'I will/want' and نخست 'first' marks priority — literally, 'From the beginning I will first want/require your pledge.'
segments.s160-g345: همه is a determiner/adverb meaning “all” placed before its objects. زیج و صرلاب are coordinated direct objects joined by و. برداشتند is a 3rd-person plural past transitive verb (برداشتن) placed after the objects, ‘they took up/removed/gathered’. The implied subject is they (omitted) and the clause reads literally “they took up all the zij and sherbet.”
segments.s160-g346: بران is a contracted/prepositional phrase meaning ‘for that/thereupon’ or ‘in regard to that (matter)’. کار here means ‘task’ or ‘business’, and یک هفته is the time phrase ‘one week’. بگذاشتند is the 3rd-person plural past of گذاشتن with the prefix بـ (here giving the sense ‘set aside / allotted’); the clause means ‘they allotted/put aside one week for that work.’
segments.s160-g347: سرانجام is an adverb meaning ‘finally’ or ‘in the end’. گفتند is the 3rd-person plural past ‘they said’; it introduces the reported question. کاین is a demonstrative ‘this/that’, and کی is the interrogative pronoun ‘who’. بود is the past-tense copula ‘was’. Together the clause reports their question ‘they finally said, “who was this?”’
segments.s160-g348: به جامی is a prepositional phrase in which به + a noun (جامی) functions as ‘with/holding a cup’ or ‘who had a cup’; جامی (indefinite) means ‘a cup/goblet’. که introduces a relative clause modifying جامی. زهر is ‘poison’ and افگنی here is adjectival/participial in sense ‘casting/causing to throw’ (i.e. ‘poison-dispensing’ or simply ‘poisonous’), so زهر افگنی describes the cup. می‌بود is the imperfect/past progressive copula ‘was’ (poetic continuous past). The whole relative clause means ‘the cup that was casting/pouring poison’ or ‘the cup that contained poison.’
segments.s160-g349: دو quantifies کودک (‘two children’). ز is the archaic/prepositional form of از meaning ‘from’ or ‘of’. پشت here functions idiomatically as ‘lineage/parentage’ or ‘from the back of’ (i.e. ‘born of’). کسی means ‘someone/another person’. دیگرند is a predicate meaning ‘they are other/belong to another’ (3rd-person plural). The clause says the two children belong to someone else (not to the expected parents).
segments.s160-g350: This clause uses a negative neither/nor construction: نه ... و نه ... . نه از پشت شاه = ‘not from the king’s lineage’ where از پشت = ‘from the back/of the lineage’. و is the coordinator ‘and’. نه زین مادرند should be read as نه از این مادرند (poetic contraction ز + این) meaning ‘nor from this mother’. مادرند is the 3rd-person plural predicate form built on مادر with -ند; here it indicates ‘they are of the mother’ (= ‘belong to this mother’) and the negation says they are not of that mother. In short: ‘neither from the king’s side nor from this mother.’
segments.s160-g351: گر is the conditional ‘if’. از introduces the source phrase ‘from/of’. گوهر here means ‘gem/jewel’ (often used figuratively for noble lineage or precious things) and شهریاران is the genitive plural ‘of the kings’. The final بدی in this position is archaic/poetic: contextually it functions as a modal/conditional marker (roughly ‘you would want/you would seek’ or ‘it would be’); its lexical senses listed in the data do not match this grammatical use, so it is treated here as an archaic verbal/conditional form.
segments.s160-g352: ازین (here written as از + ین/این) means ‘from these’ or ‘of these’ (از + این, with ین marked as an orthographic form). زیجها is the plural ‘these zij/astronomical tables’. جستن is the infinitive ‘to search/seek’. آسان is the predicate adjective ‘easy’. The final بدی again functions as an archaic/poetic conditional/modal ‘would be’ (so the clause reads ‘from these zij to seek would be easy’ → ‘it would be easy to look for [them] in these zij’).
segments.s160-g289: چو is the temporal subordinating conjunction 'when'. پیمان ستد is a compact classical sequence: ستد is past tense 'took/exacted' and governs پیمان as its object ('took the pledge'). چیز بسیار داد follows as a main-clause predicate 'gave many things' (چیز = 'things', بسیار = 'many', داد = past 'gave'). The sequence is 'When he took the pledge, he gave many things.'
segments.s160-g290: This is an imperative prohibition. سخن گفت (literally 'speak a word' / 'say anything') uses the noun سخن plus گفت (here functioning verbally as 'speak/say'); ازین در = 'about this matter' (ازین = 'of/ concerning this', در = 'matter/issue'); مکن is the negative imperative 'do not do' and is followed by هیچ یاد ('mention/remember nothing' — هیچ intensifies the prohibition, یاد here = 'mention'). The whole clause = 'do not speak/mention this at all.'
segments.s160-g291: یکی دارویی ساز is an imperative: یکی = 'some / a certain', دارویی = 'remedy/medicine', ساز = imperative 'make' (ساز here functions as 'construct/prepare'). کاین introduces a purpose clause 'that/so that this...' (ک + این = 'that this'). بفگنی is an archaic/obscure verb form meaning roughly 'you may undo/loosen/get rid of' and belongs to the subordinate purpose clause (second-person optative/subjunctive sense).
segments.s160-g292: تهی مانی is a wish/optative or jussive: تهی = 'empty, rid of', مانی (here) is not the proper-name sense but an archaic verbal form meaning 'you may remain / you may be' (the provided word senses did not include this verbal use, so it cannot be matched). و links to the next clause. راز من نشکنی is negative subjunctive/imperative: راز من = 'my secret', نشکنی = negative present/subjunctive of شکستن 'do not break' (i.e. 'do not betray/reveal'). The combined meaning: 'so that you may be rid of it and do not reveal my secret.'
segments.s160-g293: مگر here introduces a rhetorical question or challenge ('could it be that / is it that...'). کاین همه بند و چندین دروغ lists the objects: کاین = 'these/that which', همه بند = 'all these stanzas/verses', و چندین دروغ = 'and many a lie / many falsehoods'. The phrase questions whether the preceding material amounts to those things.
segments.s160-g294: بدین = به + این 'for/to this' (here 'by/for this' or 'because of this'); بچگان تو = 'your children' (بچگان = youngsters/offspring, تو = 'your'); باشد is a classical jussive/present meaning 'may be / is' (here attributive 'are' or 'serve as'); فروغ = 'splendor/glory'. Together: 'Are all these stanzas and lies, for your children, a glory?' Note: the printed segmentation separates ب and دین but they function as the preposition phrase 'بدین' = 'to/for this.'
segments.s160-g295: به کاووس گویم is a first-person statement of intention: به کاووس = 'to/for Kaavus', گویم is classical first-person present/subjunctive often used as a prospective 'I will say / I shall tell'. که introduces the content clause. این از منند literally reads 'these are from me' or 'these come from me'; منند here is the classical/cadential form meaning 'are of / like' (the lexical list gave 'like' as the available sense). The whole clause: 'I will tell Kaavus that these are from me.'
segments.s160-g296: چنین is a resumptive adverb 'thus/so'; کشته here functions as 'the slain' (a past-participle noun); بر دست = 'by the hand / in the power of' and اهریمنند is a predicate attributing them to Ahriman ('they are of the demon' or 'are in the demon's hands'). The clause describes the fate of the slain: 'thus the slain are in Ahriman's hand.'
segments.s160-g465: به دستور is a prepositional phrase 'by/at the order' placed before the verb. فرمود is a past, third-person verb (classical register) meaning 'he said/commanded.' تا introduces a subordinate clause meaning 'that'/'so that' and ساروان is the subject of that subordinate clause ('the caravan‑leaders').
segments.s160-g466: هیون آرد is the main clause of the subordinate: هیون (plural notion) is the object 'beasts/animals' and آرد is a classical third‑person verb form ('bring' / 'should bring' in the sense of a commanded action). از دشت is a locative/prepositional phrase 'from the plain', and صد کاروان is a numeral phrase 'a hundred caravans' giving the quantity/measure of what is brought.
segments.s160-g467: هیونان is the plural subject 'the beasts/animals.' The construction به هیزم کشیدن شدند uses the infinitive کشیدن ('to drag/pull') with شدند (past plural 'they became/were') to express that the animals were put/used as firewood or were dragged off for firewood; به marks purpose/role 'as/for.'
segments.s160-g468: همه شهر ایران is a distributive/collective phrase 'all the towns/cities of Iran' (classical usage can treat شهر collectively). به دیدن شدند uses به + infinitive (دیدن) + شدند to mean 'they came to see' or 'went to look on'; شدند is the past plural auxiliary.
segments.s160-g469: به صد کاروان is a prepositional/measure phrase 'with/in a hundred caravans' indicating the scale or accompaniment. اشتر سرخ موی is an appositional name plus epithet: اشتر is a proper name here, and سرخ موی ('red‑haired') is an adjectival epithet modifying that name.
segments.s160-g470: همی is an archaic durative/continuous particle 'continually/kept on'; هیزم آورد is the verb phrase 'brought firewood' (آورد classical 3rd‑person). پرخاشجوی is a postpositive adjective describing the subject ('quarrelsome/aggressive'), placed after the verb as an epithet.
segments.s160-g471: نهادند is past plural 'they placed/put' (from نهادن). هیزم دو کوه بلند is an object plus measure: 'the firewood (into) two high mountains' — کوه is used metaphorically for a heap and بلند ('high') modifies it.
segments.s160-g472: شمارش گذر کرد literally 'the counting passed' (شمارش = 'the counting/reckoning', گذر کرد = 'passed by/overflowed'); بر چون و چند is an idiomatic phrase where چون و چند means 'how and how many' i.e. particulars or exact number, so the line says the tally exceeded measure or could not be reckoned precisely.
segments.s160-g417: سپهبد is the subject ('the spahbed'). ز گفتار او is an ablative/causal prepositional phrase ('because of/from his speech'); شد is the past of شدن used copulatively ('became'); دژم is a predicative adjective placed after شد ('sullen, grim') and completes the verb phrase 'شد دژم' = 'became sullen'. Word order is typical older/poetic Persian with complement after the auxiliary verb.
segments.s160-g418: همی is an archaic/progressive marker indicating continued or immediate action here ('was/then'); زار functions adverbially meaning 'lamentingly/plaintive'; بگریست is a past-tense verb ('he wept' / 'he bewailed'); با او بهم = 'with him together' (با = with, او = him/her, بهم = together/with one another). The clause describes the spahbed's emotional reaction and links as a second coordinated clause to the previous one.
segments.s160-g419: گسی is an indefinite subject ('someone'/'a certain one'); کرد is the past of کردن used causatively ('made/caused'); سودابه را is the definite- marked object ('Sudabeh' + را); خسته‌دل is an object-complement meaning 'heart-weary / lovesick' — together 'گسی ... را خسته‌دل کرد' = 'someone made Sudabeh lovesick.'
segments.s160-g420: بران is a locative/adverbial particle 'upon that' or 'thereon'; کار is 'the affair/matter'; بنهاد is past of نهادن ('placed/instilled/committed') used here with دل as its object; پیوسته is an adverb 'continually/constantly' modifying the action; دل ('heart') is the object of بنهاد — idiomatically 'دل بنهاد' = 'to set one’s heart (on something)'.
segments.s160-g421: چنین گفت is a formulaic 'thus he said' (چنین = thus/so; گفت = said). کاندر is the fused/poetic form of 'که اندر' meaning 'that in/wherein' or introducing a subordinate locative clause; نهان means 'in secret' or 'privately'; این سخن = 'this speech/matter' (this is the topic to be examined). The phrase sets up the reported purpose clause that follows.
segments.s160-g422: پژوهیم is 1st-person-plural hortative/subjunctive (پژوهیدن) meaning 'let us investigate/examine'; تا introduces a purpose/result clause 'so that'; خود is an emphatic/reflexive 'ourselves' or 'for ourselves'; چه آید = 'what may come/what will happen' with آید (subjunctive/present) 'come/happen'; به بن literally 'to the root/base' = idiomatically 'what will come of it' or 'what will be the outcome'.
segments.s160-g423: ز پهلو here means 'from/by his side' or idiomatically 'to his side' (ز = from/at); همه موبدان را is the definite object 'all the mobeds' (همه = all); را marks them as the object; بخواند is a past tense verb here meaning 'he called/summoned' (خواند can mean 'call' in this sense). The clause describes bringing the priests to him.
segments.s160-g424: ز سودابه is a brief prepositional phrase 'about/concerning Sudabeh' (ز = from/about in poetic usage); چندی here means 'a few' or 'some' and can also mean 'for a while' depending on context; سخنها is the plural 'words/talk' (a few remarks); براند is the past of راندن ('drove/put forth/drave') used idiomatically with 'سخنها' to mean 'uttered/spoke a few words' — in poetic language verbs of motion can be used with 'سخن' to mean 'utter'.
segments.s160-g553: سیاوش is the proper-name subject. به پیش is a prepositional phrase meaning “to the presence of / before” (به + پیش), and جهاندار پاک is the noun + adjective ‘sovereign (جهاندار) pure (پاک)’ with the adjective post‑nominal as usual in Persian; the whole phrase denotes the addressee/place Siavash approaches.
segments.s160-g554: بیامد is the simple past third‑person ‘he came’ whose subject (Siavash) is understood from the previous segment. بمالید is a past tense of مالیدن (‘rub/press’); رخ را is the definite object (را marks رخ ‘face’), and به خاک is a prepositional phrase ‘to the dust/earth’ showing direction/result of the action. The two verbs are coordinated in sequence: the subject performs both actions.
segments.s160-g555: که here functions as a causal conjunction ‘for/because’ introducing the reason clause. از marks origin ‘from’, تف means ‘spit’ (a figurative or literal emission), آن کوه is ‘that mountain’, and آتش برست uses the archaic past برست (3rd sg.) meaning ‘burst forth/sprang up’, so the clause means ‘because fire sprang from the mountain’s spit’.
segments.s160-g556: همه is the quantifier ‘all/every’, کامه (from کام) means ‘desires/wishes’ modifying دشمنان ‘the enemies’ (i.e. ‘the enemies’ desires’). گشت is past of گشتن/شدن ‘became/was made’, and پست is the adjective ‘low/base’; together they say the enemies’ desires were brought low.
segments.s160-g557: بدو is the prepositional pronoun ‘to him’ (introducing the indirect object). گفت is the simple past ‘said’ (3rd sg.); شاه is the grammatical subject ‘the king’. ای دلیر جوان is a vocative address, where ای introduces the direct address ‘O brave young man’ (دلیر = brave; جوان = young man).
segments.s160-g558: که begins a relative clause describing the addressee. پاکیزه تخمی is a compact phrase: پاکیزه ‘pure/clean’ modifies تخمی (literally ‘seed‑born’, i.e. ‘of pure lineage’). و links to روشن‌روان, a compound adjective meaning ‘clear‑souled / bright‑minded’; together they further characterize the young man.
segments.s160-g559: چنانی که = ‘such that/so that’ introducing a comparative or exemplifying clause. از مادر پارسا is a source phrase: از = ‘from’, مادر = ‘mother’, پارسا = ‘pious’, so ‘from a pious mother’. The phrase sets up the origin of the person described.
segments.s160-g560: بزاید is a poetic/archaic verb form from زاد/زاییدن meaning ‘is born / is begotten’; combined with the auxiliary شود (‘becomes / will be’) it yields a future/resultive sense (‘is born who becomes’). در جهان پادشا is the predicate location/title ‘a king in the world’ (در = in, جهان = world, پادشا = king), telling the outcome of that birth.
segments.s160-g609: همی (an older Persian continuous/frequentative particle) + گفت (said) gives the sense “kept saying” or “kept thinking to himself.” با دل is literal “with the heart” = “in his heart / to himself.” که introduces the subordinate clause that continues in the next segment. بر دست شاه is a prepositional phrase meaning “in/on the king’s hand” (= under the king’s power); here دست has a figurative sense of control.
segments.s160-g610: گر introduces a conditional “if.” ایدون is an archaic/poetic verb form meaning “comes / should come to pass.” که again functions as a complementizer ‘that.’ گردَد is the present/subjunctive-like verb “become” or “turn” placed before the predicate adjective تباه “ruined/ruinous”; the clause thus means “if it should happen that Sudabeh becomes ruined.”
segments.s160-g611: به فرجام کار او is a temporal/locative phrase 'at the outcome/result of his affair.' پشیمان is an adjective 'regretful,' and شود (become) is the copular/auxiliary verb; together they mean 'he will/becomes regretful' (narrative/future sense). Word order places the circumstance before the predication.
segments.s160-g612: ز من here means 'because of me / on my account' (poetic preposition). بیند is a present/future-tense verb 'he sees' or 'he will see' with object او غم ('my grief'—او marks the experiencer in context), and چو introduces the temporal clause 'when.' پیچان is an adjectival/participial word meaning 'twisting/entangling' modifying غم, and شود completes that subordinate clause ('when [the] grief becomes entangling').
segments.s160-g613: بهانه (excuse) is the direct object placed before the verb. همی جست uses the continuous particle همی + past of جستن (جست) meaning 'kept seeking' or 'continually sought.' زان کار شاه is a genitival/ablative phrase 'from that action/affair of the king' (زان = 'from that'), indicating the source or subject of the excuse.
segments.s160-g614: بدان is here a fused adverbial/phrase (b + دان) meaning roughly 'by that / with that consideration'; تا introduces a purpose/result clause 'so that / in order that.' ببخشد is a present/jussive form 'may forgive' or simply 'forgive' used inside the purpose clause. گذشته گناه is the object 'the past sin' (گذشته qualifying گناه).
segments.s160-g615: سیاووش را uses را as the definite object/topic marker to indicate the addressee 'to Siavosh.' گفت is the reporting verb 'said' (here introducing reported speech). بخشیدمش is a past-tense verb بخشیدم 'I forgave' plus the enclitic object -ش 'him/her/it', so the reported clause reads 'I forgave him/her.'
segments.s160-g616: ازان پس که is a temporal phrase 'after that, when' (ازان = 'from that', پس = 'after'), and introduces the reason/temporal clause. خون ریختن is a verbal noun/masdar 'the shedding/spilling of blood' and functions as the object of perception; دیدمش is the speaker's past-tense 'I saw him/it' (دیدم + -ش), so the whole clause means 'after I saw blood being shed' or 'after I saw the bloodshed.'
segments.s160-g385: چو is the conditional conjunction 'if' (classical usage). خستو is an adjective 'weary/tired'; نیاید is a negative third‑person present/subjunctive form meaning 'does not come' or 'should not come'. میانش = میان + ‑ش 'between them/it', and به ار is an archaic conditional/intensifying phrase (به + ار, with ار ~ 'if/supposing') that ties the condition to the following command.
segments.s160-g386: ببرید is an imperative/hortative form of بردن ('take, carry away' — here a command). و simply links the next clause. این دانم is 'this I know' with دانم (present 'I know'). آیین و فر are two nouns stating what the speaker knows: آیین 'custom/rite/practice' and فر 'royal/divine glory' — the object of 'دانم' is supplied by apposition.
segments.s160-g387: ببردند is the past tense 'they took' (perfect/simple past). زن را uses را as the definite object marker ('the woman'). ز is the poetic preposition corresponding to modern از ('from'), and درگاه شاه = 'the king's court/presence' (درگاه = court/threshold, شاه = king).
segments.s160-g388: The clause begins with a series of instrumental/agent phrases: ز شمشیر literally 'from/by the sword' (here meaning 'by sword'). گفتند is 'they said/pronounced' and introduces what was declared. وز here should be read as و + ز ('and from/of'), linking the next items; دار is 'gallows' and چاه 'pit' — a poetic list of methods (hence the translation 'by the sword, by the gallows and the pit').
segments.s160-g389: چنین is an adverb 'thus/so' placed before the verb for emphasis. گفت is the past 'said' and jادو (here the agent) follows the verb in the literary inverted word order common in the Shahnameh. که introduces the content clause: من بی‌گناه 'I am innocent' (من = I; بی‌گناه = innocent).
segments.s160-g390: چه گویم is a rhetorical question 'what shall I say?', where گویم is the present/subjunctive form expressing 'should I say / may I say'. بدین is the prepositional phrase 'به + این' (before/at this), and نامور پیشگاه = 'this famed presence/court' (نامور 'renowned', پیشگاه 'presence/royal court') — together: 'before this renowned court.'
